1061	/-- **The slice-to-geometric equivalence functor sends `unit` to `unit`** (gap1, P1).
1062	
1063	For an open `U ⊆ X`, the functor of the slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U`
1064	(definitionally `SheafOfModules.pushforward` along `(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse` with the
1065	identity ring comparison) carries the sliced structure-sheaf module `unit (O_X.over U)` to the
1066	structure-sheaf module `unit (U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf)` of the open subscheme. This is the
1067	`F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum consumed by `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` in
1068	`overRestrictPresentation`. Project-local. -/
1069	noncomputable def overRestrictUnitIso (U : X.Opens) :
1070	    (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (SheafOfModules.unit (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)) ≅
1071	      SheafOfModules.unit U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf := by
1072	  unfold overRestrictEquiv
1073	  dsimp only [Equivalence.symm_functor]
1074	  refine (@asIso _ _ _ _ (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit
1075	      (F := (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse) (J := Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)
1076	      (S := U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf) (R := X.ringCatSheaf.over U)
1077	      (ObjectProperty.homMk (𝟙 _)))
1078	    (isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso' _ ?hpsi)).symm
1079	  exact inferInstanceAs (IsIso (𝟙 U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf))

1081	/-- **Slice presentation ⟹ geometric-restriction presentation** (gap1, P1).
1082	
1083	Given a sheaf of modules `M` on `X`, an open `U ⊆ X`, and a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1084	abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U`, there is a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1085	*geometric* restriction `(pullback U.ι).obj M = U.ι^* M` on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`. The
1086	transport is `Presentation.map` along the slice-to-geometric equivalence functor (using the unit-iso
1087	`overRestrictUnitIso`) followed by `Presentation.ofIsIso` across the bridge
1088	`overRestrictPullbackIso` (gap1, C).
1089	
1090	This closes the slice-touching step of the gap1 per-element transport
1091	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1): with `U = q.X i` and
1092	`P = q.presentation i` it produces a global presentation of `U.ι^* M`; the remaining geometric step
1093	restricts further to a basic affine `D(r) ≅ Spec R_r` and concludes via
1094	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`. Project-local. -/
1095	noncomputable def overRestrictPresentation (U : X.Opens) (M : X.Modules)
1096	    (P : (M.over U).Presentation) : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M).Presentation :=
1097	  SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u} (overRestrictPullbackIso U M).hom
1098	    (SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u} P (overRestrictEquiv U).functor (overRestrictUnitIso U))

1120	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M).Presentation :=
1121	  overRestrictPresentation (q.X i) M (q.presentation i)

1127	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — basic-open presentation descent (gap1, P1 keystone)
1128	
1129	This section assembles the gap1 per-element keystone
1130	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent`
1131	(`isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen`): on a basic open `D(r)` contained in a cover member
1132	`q.X i` of the quasi-coherence data, the restricted sheaf `M|_{D(r)}` is a geometric tilde, i.e.
1133	its `fromTildeΓ` counit is an isomorphism.
1134	
1135	The route follows the affine descent of the recipe, building on the slice-to-geometric presentation
1136	transport of the previous section:
1137	
1138	1. `presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i` is a *global* `Presentation` of the geometric
1139	   restriction `N := (q.X i).ι^* M` on the open subscheme `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`.
1140	2. For any open `W ⊆ Z`, the global presentation `PN` slices to a slice presentation `N.over W` via
1141	   the single `Presentation.map` of the over-functor `pushforward (𝟙 …)` (the
1142	   `QuasicoherentData.ofPresentation` template — no iterated-slice equivalence is needed because
1143	   `PN` is already a global presentation on the genuine scheme `Z`).
1144	3. `overRestrictPresentation W N PNW` transports it to a global presentation of the geometric
1145	   restriction `(pullback W.ι).obj N` on the open subscheme `W.toScheme`.
1146	4. For `W` *affine*, `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec` identifies `W.toScheme ≅ Spec Γ(Z, W)`; transporting the
1147	   presentation across this iso (whose `Opens.map` is `Final`, so `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an iso)
1148	   lands a global presentation on the genuine affine `Spec Γ(Z, W)`.
1149	5. A global presentation forces `fromTildeΓ` to be an isomorphism
1150	   (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`).
1151	
1152	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` essential-image bridge; this descent
1153	is project-local. -/

1190	the inverse-image functor `Opens.map φ.inv.base : Opens ↥Y ⥤ Opens ↥Z` is an equivalence (with
1191	inverse `Opens.map φ.hom.base`), assembled from the pseudofunctoriality isos `Opens.mapComp` /
1192	`Opens.mapId`. Its purpose is to supply the `Final` instance that makes `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` an
1193	isomorphism in `pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso`. Project-local. -/
1194	noncomputable def opensMapEquivOfIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
1195	    TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y ≌ TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Z where
1196	  functor := Opens.map φ.inv.base
1197	  inverse := Opens.map φ.hom.base
1198	  unitIso := (Opens.mapId _).symm ≪≫
1199	      Opens.mapIso (𝟙 _) (φ.hom.base ≫ φ.inv.base)
1200	        (show (𝟙 _) = φ.hom.base ≫ φ.inv.base by
1201	          rw [← AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.comp_base, φ.hom_inv_id]; rfl) ≪≫
1202	      Opens.mapComp φ.hom.base φ.inv.base

1204	      Opens.mapIso (φ.inv.base ≫ φ.hom.base) (𝟙 _)
1205	        (show φ.inv.base ≫ φ.hom.base = 𝟙 _ by
1206	          rw [← AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.comp_base, φ.inv_hom_id]; rfl) ≪≫
1207	      Opens.mapId _

1315	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — gap1-D: the section-localization descent
1316	
1317	The keystone `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` reduces (Hartshorne II.5.3 / Stacks
1318	`lemma-invert-f-sections`) to a finite-cover sheaf-gluing argument.  The single geometric input
1319	is the **per-cover-element** fact that on each `D(r)` of a finite cover `{D(r_j)}` of `Spec R`
1320	(with `D(r_j) ≤ q.X i`), the basic-open restriction `Γ(M, D(r)) → Γ(M, D(f) ⊓ D(r))` is a
1321	localization at `powers f` — this is exactly the P1 local-tilde data transported to sections, and
1322	is the gated hypothesis `Hfr` below.  Given `Hfr` (for every `r` whose `D(r)` sits inside a cover
1323	member, hence also for the overlaps `D(r r')`), the descent is pure sheaf theory: separatedness
1324	gives the `exists_of_eq` field, gluing the patched compatible family gives `surj'`, and the global
1325	`map_units` field holds for arbitrary `M` (`map_units_restrict_basicOpen`). -/
1326	
1327	/-- A finite family `t` spanning `R` gives a basic-open cover of `Spec R`: the supremum of the
1328	`D(r)` over `r ∈ t` is `⊤`.  Project-local glue feeding the sheaf-gluing reduction of the
1329	section-localization descent. -/
1330	private lemma iSup_basicOpen_subtype_eq_top {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {t : Finset R}
1331	    (hspan : Ideal.span (t : Set R) = ⊤) :
1332	    (⨆ r : {x // x ∈ t}, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r : R) : (Spec R).Opens)) = ⊤ := by
1333	  rw [iSup_subtype]
1334	  have h := (PrimeSpectrum.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top_iff' (s := (t : Set R))).mpr hspan
1335	  simpa using h
1336	
1337	/-- Restriction maps of `modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M` compose: restricting `A → B → C` equals the
1338	direct restriction `A → C`.  Poset-hom uniqueness makes the two intermediate morphisms compose to
1339	the direct one.  Project-local bookkeeping for the section-localization descent. -/
1340	private lemma res_comp {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
1341	    (F : TopCat.Sheaf (ModuleCat.{u} ↑R) ↑(Spec R).toPresheafedSpace)
1342	    {A B C : (Spec R).Opens} (hBA : B ≤ A) (hCB : C ≤ B) (hCA : C ≤ A)
1343	    (y : ToType (F.presheaf.obj (.op A))) :
1344	    (F.presheaf.map (homOfLE hCB).op).hom ((F.presheaf.map (homOfLE hBA).op).hom y)
1345	      = (F.presheaf.map (homOfLE hCA).op).hom y := by
1346	  rw [← ModuleCat.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp]; rfl
1347	
1348	/-- **Separatedness/torsion field of the section-localization descent.**  Given the
1349	per-cover-element localization data `Hfr` (on each `D(r)` of a finite basic-open cover `{D(r)}` of
1350	`Spec R`, the restriction `Γ(M, D(r)) → Γ(M, D(f) ⊓ D(r))` is a localization at `powers f`), any
1351	global section `x` that restricts to `0` on `D(f)` is killed by a power of `f`.  This is the

1615	`Γ(M, U) → Γ(M, D(f) ⊓ U)` is a localization at `powers f` (`Hfr` — the gated P1 local-tilde data).

1618	This is the project-internal finite-equalizer/flatness descent of Stacks `lemma-invert-f-sections`
1619	(Hartshorne II.5.3), built without the global affine `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` equivalence (which is
1620	gap1 itself): `map_units` holds for arbitrary `M` (`map_units_restrict_basicOpen`), `surj'` is
1621	`descent_surj`, and `exists_of_eq` is `descent_smul_eq_zero`.  The named gap1 keystone
1622	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` for quasi-coherent `M` is this lemma instantiated at the cover
1623	`exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData` once `Hfr` is produced from
1624	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen` (the slice→Spec-`R_r` section transport, the remaining gated
1625	step).  Project-local. -/
1626	theorem isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_cover {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : (Spec R).Modules)
1627	    (f : R) (t : Finset R) (hspan : Ideal.span (t : Set R) = ⊤)
1628	    (Hfr : ∀ U : (Spec R).Opens, (∃ r ∈ t, U ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r) →
1629	      IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
1630	        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
1631	          (homOfLE (inf_le_right : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ U ≤ U)).op).hom) :
1632	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
1633	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
1634	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom where
1635	  map_units := map_units_restrict_basicOpen M f
1636	  surj y := by
1637	    obtain ⟨x, n, hxn⟩ := descent_surj M f t hspan Hfr y
1638	    exact ⟨⟨x, ⟨f ^ n, n, rfl⟩⟩, hxn⟩
1639	  exists_of_eq {x₁ x₂} h := by
1640	    have hθ : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
1641	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom (x₁ - x₂) = 0 := by
1642	      rw [map_sub, h, sub_self]
1643	    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := descent_smul_eq_zero M f t hspan
1644	      (fun r hr => Hfr (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r) ⟨r, hr, le_refl _⟩) (x₁ - x₂) hθ
1645	    exact ⟨⟨f ^ n, n, rfl⟩, sub_eq_zero.mp ((smul_sub (f ^ n) x₁ x₂).symm.trans hn)⟩
1646	
1647	end Scheme.Modules
1648	
1649	end BasicOpenPresentationDescent
1650	
1651	end AlgebraicGeometry

819	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the Grassmannian glue data
820	
821	We assemble the affine charts, their principal-open overlaps, the transition
822	isomorphisms and the cocycle into `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData`, indexed by
823	the size-`d` subsets `{I : Finset (Fin r) // I.card = d}`. Its `.glued` is the
824	Grassmannian scheme `Gr(d,r)` over `ℤ`.
825	
826	Blueprint reference: `def:gr_glued_scheme`
827	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
828	
829	/-- The triple-overlap `t'`-field of the Grassmannian glue data
830	(`def:gr_glued_scheme`): the morphism
831	`U^I_J ×_{U^I} U^I_K ⟶ U^J_K ×_{U^J} U^J_I` reconciling the two pullbacks via the
832	away-pullback identification, the localised transition `Θ_{I,J}`, and the
833	order-swap isomorphism. Project-local. -/
834	noncomputable def chartTransition' (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
835	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
836	    Limits.pullback (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK) ⟶
837	      Limits.pullback (chartIncl d r J K hJ hK) (chartIncl d r J I hJ hI) :=
838	  (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).hom ≫
839	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK)) ≫
840	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
841	      (awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).toRingHom) ≫
842	    (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).inv
843	
844	/-- `awayMulCommEquiv` lies over the base ring: it intertwines the two structure maps
845	`R → R[1/(xy)]` and `R → R[1/(yx)]`. Project-local helper for the `t_fac`/cocycle
846	ring computations. -/
847	lemma awayMulCommEquiv_comp_algebraMap {A : Type*} [CommRing A] (x y : A) :
848	    (awayMulCommEquiv x y).toRingHom.comp (algebraMap A (Localization.Away (x * y)))
849	      = algebraMap A (Localization.Away (y * x)) := by
850	  haveI : IsLocalization.Away (y * x) (Localization.Away (x * y)) := by
851	    rw [mul_comm y x]; infer_instance
852	  ext a
853	  exact (IsLocalization.algEquiv (Submonoid.powers (y * x))
854	    (Localization.Away (x * y)) (Localization.Away (y * x))).commutes a
855	
856	/-- The ring-hom identity underlying the `t_fac` coherence field of the Grassmannian
857	glue data: over the triple-overlap rings, the localised transition `Θ_{I,J}`
858	pre-composed with the order-swap and right inclusion equals the left inclusion
859	post-composed with the plain transition `θ_{I,J}`. Both reduce to
860	`ι^L ∘ θ̃_{I,J}`. Project-local. -/
861	theorem chartTransition'_ringIdentity (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
862	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
863	    (cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
864	        ((awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).toRingHom.comp
865	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)))
866	      = (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
867	          (transitionMap d r I J hI hJ) := by
868	  apply IsLocalization.ringHom_ext (Submonoid.powers (minorDet d r J I hJ hI))
869	  -- Reduce both sides to `ι^L ∘ θ̃_{I,J}` after precomposing with the structure map of
870	  -- `R^J[1/P^J_I]`.
871	  rw [RingHom.comp_assoc, RingHom.comp_assoc, awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap,
872	    awayMulCommEquiv_comp_algebraMap,
873	    cocycleΘIJ, IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
874	    transitionMap, IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp]
875	
876	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
877	-- The `erw` through the `HasPullback` instance diamond on the heavy `MvPolynomial`
878	-- localisation objects is defeq-expensive; the raised limit covers it.
879	/-- The `t_fac`-compatibility field of the Grassmannian glue data
880	(`def:gr_glued_scheme`): the triple-overlap transition `t'` is compatible with the
881	projections, `t'_{I,J,K} ≫ pr₂ = pr₁ ≫ t_{I,J}`. Reduces, both pullbacks being
882	affine, to the ring identity `chartTransition'_ringIdentity` via the leg lemmas.
883	Project-local. -/
884	theorem chartTransition'_fac (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
885	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
886	    chartTransition' d r I J K hI hJ hK ≫
887	        Limits.pullback.snd (chartIncl d r J K hJ hK) (chartIncl d r J I hJ hI)
888	      = Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK) ≫
889	          chartTransition d r I J hI hJ := by
890	  -- Rewrite the source `pr₁` via the iso (keeping a single `awayPullbackIso` term so the
891	  -- `HasPullback` instance is consistent), then cancel the leg via the iso identity.
892	  have hfstc : (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).inv ≫
893	        Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK)
894	      = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
895	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))) :=
896	    awayPullbackIso_inv_fst _ _
897	  have hfst := (Iso.inv_comp_eq _).mp hfstc
898	  -- The pure ring/`Spec` content: the three localised pieces equal the plain transition.
899	  have hXY : Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK)) ≫
900	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
901	          (awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).toRingHom) ≫
902	          Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
903	            (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)))
904	      = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
905	            (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))) ≫
906	          Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (transitionMap d r I J hI hJ)) := by
907	    rw [← Spec.map_comp, ← Spec.map_comp, ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp,
908	      ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, chartTransition'_ringIdentity]
909	  rw [hfst, chartTransition']
910	  simp only [Category.assoc]
911	  -- `erw` (defeq) to fire the snd-leg lemma through the `HasPullback` instance diamond.
912	  erw [awayPullbackIso_inv_snd]
913	  simp only [chartTransition]
914	  -- `congrArg` + defeq associativity closes it (syntactic `rw`/`Category.assoc` are blocked
915	  -- by the Scheme-category instance diamond on these heavy localisation objects).
916	  exact congrArg (_ ≫ ·) hXY

918	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the rotated triple-overlap ring cocycle `Φ = id`
919	
920	The `cocycle` field of the Grassmannian glue data reduces (after stripping the conjugating
921	pullback isomorphisms) to a single ring identity `Φ = id` over the triple-overlap ring
922	`S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]`, where
923	`Φ := Θ_{I,J,K} ∘ swap_J ∘ Θ_{J,K,I} ∘ swap_K ∘ Θ_{K,I,J} ∘ swap_I` (rotated index triples).
924	We prove it by telescoping with the cocycle condition `cocycleCondition` (collapsing
925	`θ_{I,J} ∘ θ_{J,K} = θ_{I,K}`) down to a single inverse pair `θ_{I,K} ∘ θ_{K,I} = id`,
926	which is closed by the matrix computation `transitionInvImageMatrix` (the `(Y_K)⁻¹ Y` collapse
927	of `cocycle_imageMatrix_eq`, run for the inverse pair).
928	
929	Blueprint reference: `lem:gr_cocycle_phi_id`
930	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
931	
932	/-- The order-swap absorbs a left away-inclusion into a right one:
933	`swap_{x,y} ∘ ι^L_{x,y} = ι^R_{y,x}` as maps `R[1/x] → R[1/(yx)]`. Project-local helper for
934	the rotation lemma `rotMid`. -/
935	lemma awayMulCommEquiv_comp_awayInclLeft {A : Type*} [CommRing A] (x y : A) :
936	    (awayMulCommEquiv x y).toRingHom.comp (awayInclLeft x y) = awayInclRight y x := by
937	  apply IsLocalization.ringHom_ext (Submonoid.powers x)
938	  rw [RingHom.comp_assoc, awayInclLeft_comp_algebraMap, awayMulCommEquiv_comp_algebraMap,
939	    awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap]
940	
941	/-- **Rotation lemma** for the triple-overlap transitions: conjugating the rotated
942	`Θ_{J,K,I}` by the two order-swaps recovers the `J,K`-transition `Θ_{J,K}` in the `I,J,K`
943	frame. Both sides are lifts of `θ̃_{J,K}`; checked on the chart ring `R^K`. Project-local. -/
944	private lemma rotMid (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
945	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
946	    ((awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).toRingHom.comp
947	        (cocycleΘIJ d r J K I hJ hK hI)).comp
948	        (awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r K I hK hI) (minorDet d r K J hK hJ)).toRingHom
949	      = cocycleΘJK d r I J K hI hJ hK := by
950	  apply IsLocalization.ringHom_ext
951	    (Submonoid.powers (minorDet d r K I hK hI * minorDet d r K J hK hJ))
952	  rw [RingHom.comp_assoc, awayMulCommEquiv_comp_algebraMap, RingHom.comp_assoc, cocycleΘIJ,
953	    IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp, ← RingHom.comp_assoc, awayMulCommEquiv_comp_awayInclLeft,
954	    cocycleΘJK, IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp]
955	
956	/-- The matrix collapse behind the inverse pair `θ_{I,K} ∘ θ_{K,I} = id`: pushing the image
957	matrix `(X^K_I)⁻¹ X^K` of `θ_{K,I}` forward along `θ_{I,K}` (realised as
958	`cocycleΘIK ∘ ι^L`) recovers the universal matrix `X^I` over the triple-overlap ring `S_I`.
959	Both reduce to `W = X^I` over `S_I` via the `(W_K)⁻¹ W` computation, using `W_I = 1`.
960	Project-local. -/
961	private lemma transitionInvImageMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
962	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
963	    (imageMatrix d r K I hK hI).map
964	        ((cocycleΘIK d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
965	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r K I hK hI) (minorDet d r K J hK hJ)))
966	      = (universalMatrix d r I hI).map
967	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
968	            (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK))) := by
969	  set incl := (cocycleΘIK d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
970	    (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r K I hK hI) (minorDet d r K J hK hJ)) with hincldef
971	  have hcomp : incl.comp (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ K}) ℤ)
972	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r K I hK hI)))
973	      = (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
974	          (transitionPreMap d r I K hI hK).toRingHom := by
975	    rw [hincldef, RingHom.comp_assoc, awayInclLeft_comp_algebraMap, cocycleΘIK,
976	      IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp]
977	  set W := (universalMatrix d r I hI).map
978	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
979	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK))) with hW
980	  have hWK : IsUnit (W.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r))).det := by
981	    have e : (W.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r))).det
982	        = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
983	            (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK))
984	            (minorDet d r I K hI hK) := by
985	      rw [hW, Matrix.submatrix_map]
986	      exact (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
987	    rw [e]; exact isUnit_algebraMap_away_right _ _
988	  have hMK : (universalMatrix d r K hK).map
989	        ((awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
990	          (transitionPreMap d r I K hI hK).toRingHom)
991	      = (W.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r)))⁻¹ * W := by
992	    have e1 : (universalMatrix d r K hK).map
993	          ((awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
994	            (transitionPreMap d r I K hI hK).toRingHom)
995	        = (imageMatrix d r I K hI hK).map
996	            (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) := by
997	      rw [← map_map_eq_of_comp (universalMatrix d r K hK)
998	          (transitionPreMap d r I K hI hK).toRingHom
999	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) _ rfl]
1000	      congr 1
1001	      exact universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap d r I K hI hK
1002	    rw [e1, imageMatrix_map_eq d r I K hI hK
1003	      (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
1004	      (awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap _ _), hW]
1005	  have hWI : W.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
1006	    rw [hW, Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_submatrix_self]
1007	    exact Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _)
1008	  have hmm : (imageMatrix d r K I hK hI).map incl
1009	      = (universalMinorInv d r K I hK hI).map incl
1010	        * ((universalMatrix d r K hK).map
1011	            (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ K}) ℤ)
1012	              (Localization.Away (minorDet d r K I hK hI)))).map incl := by
1013	    rw [imageMatrix]; exact Matrix.map_mul
1014	  rw [hmm, map_map_eq_of_comp _ _ _ _ hcomp, hMK, universalMinorInv,
1015	    ← map_nonsing_inv incl (universalMinor d r K I hK hI) (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r K I hK hI),
1016	    universalMinor, map_map_eq_of_comp _ _ _ _ hcomp, ← Matrix.submatrix_map, hMK,
1017	    mul_submatrix_col (W.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r)))⁻¹ W
1018	      (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)),
1019	    hWI, mul_one, Matrix.nonsing_inv_nonsing_inv _ hWK, ← Matrix.mul_assoc,
1020	    Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv _ hWK, Matrix.one_mul]
1021	
1022	/-- The inverse-pair ring identity `θ_{I,K} ∘ θ_{K,I} = id` over the triple-overlap ring
1023	`S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]`, phrased through the localised transitions and the order-swap:
1024	`Θ_{I,J,K} ∘ Θ_{K,I,J} ∘ swap_I = id`. Closed on chart-ring generators by the matrix
1025	collapse `transitionInvImageMatrix`. Project-local. -/
1026	private lemma transitionInvPair (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1027	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
1028	    (cocycleΘIK d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
1029	        ((cocycleΘIJ d r K I J hK hI hJ).comp
1030	          (awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).toRingHom)
1031	      = RingHom.id (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK)) := by
1032	  have hLHScomp : ((cocycleΘIK d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
1033	        ((cocycleΘIJ d r K I J hK hI hJ).comp
1034	          (awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).toRingHom)).comp
1035	        (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1036	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK)))
1037	      = ((cocycleΘIK d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
1038	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r K I hK hI) (minorDet d r K J hK hJ))).comp
1039	          (transitionPreMap d r K I hK hI).toRingHom := by
1040	    rw [RingHom.comp_assoc, RingHom.comp_assoc, awayMulCommEquiv_comp_algebraMap, cocycleΘIJ,
1041	      IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp, ← RingHom.comp_assoc]
1042	  apply IsLocalization.ringHom_ext
1043	    (Submonoid.powers (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK))
1044	  rw [RingHom.id_comp, hLHScomp]
1045	  apply MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext
1046	  · intro n
1047	    exact RingHom.congr_fun (RingHom.ext_int
1048	      (((cocycleΘIK d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
1049	        (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r K I hK hI) (minorDet d r K J hK hJ))).comp
1050	          ((transitionPreMap d r K I hK hI).toRingHom.comp MvPolynomial.C))
1051	      ((algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1052	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK))).comp
1053	            MvPolynomial.C)) n
1054	  · intro e
1055	    have h := congrFun (congrFun (transitionInvImageMatrix d r I J K hI hJ hK) e.1) e.2.1
1056	    rw [Matrix.map_apply, Matrix.map_apply, universalMatrix, dif_neg e.2.2] at h
1057	    simpa [RingHom.comp_apply, transitionPreMap, MvPolynomial.aeval_X] using h

1059	/-- **The rotated triple-overlap ring cocycle** `Φ = id` (`lem:gr_cocycle_phi_id`): over the
1060	triple-overlap ring `S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]`, the composite
1061	`Θ_{I,J,K} ∘ swap_J ∘ Θ_{J,K,I} ∘ swap_K ∘ Θ_{K,I,J} ∘ swap_I` is the identity. This is the
1062	ring identity underlying the `cocycle` field of the Grassmannian glue data. Proved by
1063	telescoping: the middle `swap_J ∘ Θ_{J,K,I} ∘ swap_K` collapses to `Θ_{J,K}` (`rotMid`), then
1064	`Θ_{I,J} ∘ Θ_{J,K} = Θ_{I,K}` (`cocycleCondition`), leaving the inverse pair
1065	`Θ_{I,K} ∘ Θ_{K,I} ∘ swap_I = id` (`transitionInvPair`). -/
1066	theorem cocyclePhiId (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1067	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
1068	    (cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
1069	        ((awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).toRingHom.comp
1070	          ((cocycleΘIJ d r J K I hJ hK hI).comp
1071	            ((awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r K I hK hI) (minorDet d r K J hK hJ)).toRingHom.comp
1072	              ((cocycleΘIJ d r K I J hK hI hJ).comp
1073	                (awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).toRingHom))))
1074	      = RingHom.id (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK)) := by
1075	  have hΦ : (cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
1076	        ((awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).toRingHom.comp

1107	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
1108	    chartTransition' d r I J K hI hJ hK ≫ chartTransition' d r J K I hJ hK hI ≫
1109	        chartTransition' d r K I J hK hI hJ
1110	      = CategoryTheory.CategoryStruct.id
1111	          (Limits.pullback (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK)) := by
1112	  have h6 : Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK)) ≫
1113	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)
1114	          (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).toRingHom) ≫
1115	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (cocycleΘIJ d r J K I hJ hK hI)) ≫

1130	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the Grassmannian glue data and scheme
1131	
1132	Assemble the affine charts, principal-open overlaps, transition isomorphisms, and the
1133	cocycle into `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData`, indexed by the size-`d` subsets
1134	`{I : Finset (Fin r) // I.card = d}`; its `.glued` is the Grassmannian scheme `Gr(d,r)`
1135	over `ℤ`. Blueprint reference: `def:gr_glued_scheme`. -/
1136	
1137	/-- The **Grassmannian glue data** (`def:gr_glued_scheme`): the `Scheme.GlueData` whose
1138	charts are `affineChart`, overlaps `chartOverlap`, inclusions `chartIncl`, transitions
1139	`chartTransition`, with cocycle `chartTransition'_cocycle`. Indexed by the size-`d` subsets
1140	of `Fin r`. Project-local. -/
1141	noncomputable def theGlueData (d r : ℕ) : Scheme.GlueData where
1142	  J := {I : Finset (Fin r) // I.card = d}
1143	  U I := affineChart d r I.1
1144	  V p := chartOverlap d r p.1.1 p.2.1 p.1.2 p.2.2
1145	  f I J := chartIncl d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2
1146	  f_id I := chartIncl_self_isIso d r I.1 I.2
1147	  f_open I J := chartIncl_isOpenImmersion d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2
1148	  t I J := chartTransition d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2
1149	  t_id I := chartTransition_self d r I.1 I.2
1150	  t' I J K := chartTransition' d r I.1 J.1 K.1 I.2 J.2 K.2
1151	  t_fac I J K := chartTransition'_fac d r I.1 J.1 K.1 I.2 J.2 K.2
1152	  cocycle I J K := chartTransition'_cocycle d r I.1 J.1 K.1 I.2 J.2 K.2
1153	
1154	/-- The **Grassmannian scheme** `Gr(d,r)` over `ℤ` (`def:gr_glued_scheme`): the scheme glued
1155	from the `Nat.choose r d` affine charts `U^I` along the Plücker transition isomorphisms.
1156	Project-local. -/
1157	noncomputable def scheme (d r : ℕ) : Scheme :=
1158	  (theGlueData d r).glued
1159	
1160	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the restricted diagonal and separatedness
1161	
1162	Following Nitsure §1, "Separatedness": the Grassmannian is separated over `ℤ` because on
1163	each affine patch `U^I ×_ℤ U^J` of `Gr(d,r) ×_ℤ Gr(d,r)` the restricted diagonal is a
1164	closed immersion, equivalently the comorphism ring map
1165	`δ_{I,J} : ℤ[X^I] ⊗_ℤ ℤ[X^J] → R^I_J = ℤ[X^I, 1/P^I_J]`,
1166	`X^I ⊗ 1 ↦ X^I`, `1 ⊗ X^J ↦ (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` (the comorphism `θ_{I,J}`), is surjective.
1167	We build `δ_{I,J}` as the tensor-product lift of the structure map `R^I → R^I_J` and the
1168	pre-localisation transition hom `θ̃_{I,J}`, and prove it surjective: its image contains
1169	`X^I` (left factor) and `1/P^I_J = δ_{I,J}(1 ⊗ P^J_I)` (the `I`-minor determinant of
1170	`(X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`), hence all of `R^I_J`.
1171	
1172	Blueprint reference: `lem:gr_separated`
1173	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
1174	
1175	/-- The pre-hom sends `P^J_I` to the multiplicative inverse of (the image of) `P^I_J`:
1176	`θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_I) · P^I_J = 1` in `R^I_J`. This refines `isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet`
1177	to the explicit two-sided inverse, used to realise `1/P^I_J` in the image of the diagonal
1178	ring map. Project-local. -/
1179	theorem transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1180	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
1181	    transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) *
1182	        algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1183	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) = 1 := by
1184	  have e1 : transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)
1185	      = (((universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id
1186	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))).map
1187	            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)).det :=
1188	    RingHom.map_det (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom _
1189	  rw [e1, ← Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap, imageMatrix_submatrix_I]
1190	  have hu : (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det
1191	      = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1192	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
1193	    (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
1194	  rw [← hu, ← Matrix.det_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1, Matrix.det_one]
1195	
1196	/-- The **restricted-diagonal ring map** `δ_{I,J} : ℤ[X^I] ⊗_ℤ ℤ[X^J] → R^I_J`
1197	(`lem:gr_separated`): the comorphism of the restricted diagonal
1198	`Δ^{-1}(U^I ×_ℤ U^J) = U^I_J → U^I ×_ℤ U^J`. It is the tensor-product lift of the
1199	structure map `R^I → R^I_J` (first factor) and the pre-localisation transition hom
1200	`θ̃_{I,J} : R^J → R^I_J` (second factor), so `X^I ⊗ 1 ↦ X^I` and
1201	`1 ⊗ X^J ↦ (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`. Project-local. -/
1202	noncomputable def diagonalRingMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1203	    (hJ : J.card = d) :=
1204	  Algebra.TensorProduct.lift
1205	    (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom ℤ (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1206	      (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))
1207	    (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)
1208	    (fun _ _ => Commute.all _ _)
1209	
1210	/-- `δ_{I,J}` on the left factor is the structure map: `δ_{I,J}(a ⊗ 1) = a` in `R^I_J`.
1211	Project-local. -/
1212	theorem diagonalRingMap_left (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1213	    (hJ : J.card = d) (a : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) :
1214	    diagonalRingMap d r I J hI hJ (a ⊗ₜ[ℤ] 1)
1215	      = algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) a := by
1216	  rw [diagonalRingMap, Algebra.TensorProduct.lift_tmul, map_one, mul_one]
1217	  rfl
1218	
1219	/-- `δ_{I,J}` on the right factor is the pre-localisation transition hom:
1220	`δ_{I,J}(1 ⊗ b) = θ̃_{I,J}(b)`. Project-local. -/
1221	theorem diagonalRingMap_right (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1222	    (hJ : J.card = d) (b : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ) :
1223	    diagonalRingMap d r I J hI hJ (1 ⊗ₜ[ℤ] b) = transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ b := by
1224	  rw [diagonalRingMap, Algebra.TensorProduct.lift_tmul, map_one, one_mul]
1225	
1226	/-- The **restricted-diagonal ring map is surjective** (`lem:gr_separated`): the comorphism
1227	`δ_{I,J} : ℤ[X^I] ⊗_ℤ ℤ[X^J] → R^I_J` of the restricted diagonal is surjective, so the
1228	restricted diagonal `U^I_J → U^I ×_ℤ U^J` is a closed immersion. The image contains the
1229	structure-map image of `R^I` (left factor) and `1/P^I_J = δ_{I,J}(1 ⊗ P^J_I)` (right
1230	factor), which together generate `R^I_J = R^I[1/P^I_J]`. Project-local. -/
1231	theorem diagonalRingMap_surjective (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1232	    (hJ : J.card = d) : Function.Surjective (diagonalRingMap d r I J hI hJ) := by
1233	  intro z
1234	  -- `z · uⁿ = algebraMap a` for some `a` and `n = ` power of `P^I_J`, by the localisation
1235	  -- property, where `u := P^I_J` in `R^I_J`.
1236	  obtain ⟨⟨a, s⟩, hs⟩ := IsLocalization.surj (Submonoid.powers (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) z
1237	  obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := s.2
1238	  -- The witness pushes the power into the second tensor factor: `a ⊗ (P^J_I)ⁿ`, avoiding
1239	  -- any arithmetic in the tensor-product ring.
1240	  refine ⟨a ⊗ₜ[ℤ] (minorDet d r J I hJ hI ^ n), ?_⟩
1241	  -- `v := θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_I)` is the inverse of `u := P^I_J` in `R^I_J`.
1242	  have hvu : transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) *
1243	      algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1244	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) = 1 :=
1245	    transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul d r I J hI hJ
1246	  -- `algebraMap ↑s = uⁿ`.
1247	  have hsu : algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1248	      (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (s : _)
1249	      = (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1250	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) ^ n := by
1251	    rw [← hn, map_pow]
1252	  -- `algebraMap a = z · uⁿ`.
1253	  have key : algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1254	      (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) a
1255	      = z * (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1256	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) ^ n := by
1257	    rw [← hs, hsu]
1258	  -- `δ(a ⊗ (P^J_I)ⁿ) = algebraMap a · vⁿ = z · uⁿ · vⁿ = z · (u·v)ⁿ = z`.
1259	  rw [diagonalRingMap, Algebra.TensorProduct.lift_tmul, map_pow,
1260	    IsScalarTower.coe_toAlgHom', key, mul_assoc, ← mul_pow, mul_comm _
1261	      (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)), hvu, one_pow, mul_one]
1262	
1263	/-- The intersection pullback `U^I ∩ U^J = pullback (ι i) (ι j)` is the chart overlap
1264	`U^I_J = Spec R^I[1/P^I_J]` (`lem:gr_separated`, source identification): from the glue-data
1265	universal property `theGlueData.vPullbackConeIsLimit`, whose cone point is `V (i,j) = chartOverlap`.
1266	This is the `e₂` source iso of the restricted-diagonal closed-immersion argument. Project-local. -/
1267	noncomputable def pullbackιIso (d r : ℕ) (i j : (theGlueData d r).J) :
1268	    Limits.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι i) ((theGlueData d r).ι j) ≅
1269	      chartOverlap d r i.1 j.1 i.2 j.2 :=
1270	  (Limits.limit.isLimit _).conePointUniqueUpToIso
1271	    ((theGlueData d r).vPullbackConeIsLimit i j)

1273	/-! ### Separatedness of the Grassmannian scheme
1274	
1275	The blueprint target `lem:gr_separated` (`Grassmannian.isSeparated : (scheme d r).IsSeparated`)
1276	is proved via the **structure morphism** `toSpecZ : scheme d r ⟶ Spec ℤ` (`scheme` is a
1277	`Scheme.{0}`, so `Spec ℤ` is genuinely terminal — `specZIsTerminal` — and `toSpecZ` is its unique
1278	map). We show `IsSeparated toSpecZ` by the Proj template (`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.isSeparated`,
1279	`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/Proper.lean`): on each patch `U^I ×_ℤ U^J` of the
1280	cover `Pullback.openCoverOfLeftRight (theGlueData d r).openCover (theGlueData d r).openCover`,

1284	
1285	/-- The **structure morphism** `Gr(d,r) → Spec ℤ` (`def:gr_glued_scheme`): the unique morphism to
1286	the terminal object `Spec ℤ` (the Grassmannian scheme is a `Scheme.{0}`, so `specZIsTerminal`
1287	applies). Project-local: the genuine base over which the Grassmannian is separated and proper. -/
1288	noncomputable def toSpecZ (d r : ℕ) : scheme d r ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of ℤ) :=
1289	  specZIsTerminal.from (scheme d r)

1291	/-- The chart inclusion composed with the structure morphism is the affine structure map

1297	          (algebraMap ℤ (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ i.1}) ℤ))) :=
1298	  specZIsTerminal.hom_ext _ _

1307	  have := (Limits.limit.isLimit
1308	      (Limits.cospan ((theGlueData d r).ι i)
1309	        ((theGlueData d r).ι j))).conePointUniqueUpToIso_inv_comp
1310	    ((theGlueData d r).vPullbackConeIsLimit i j) Limits.WalkingCospan.left

1317	    (pullbackιIso d r i j).inv ≫

1320	  have := (Limits.limit.isLimit
1321	      (Limits.cospan ((theGlueData d r).ι i)

1323	    ((theGlueData d r).vPullbackConeIsLimit i j) Limits.WalkingCospan.right

1329	Both reduce to `θ̃_{I,J} = θ_{I,J} ∘ (R^J → R^J_I)` (`IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp`).

1331	theorem chartTransition_comp_chartIncl (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1332	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
1333	    chartTransition d r I J hI hJ ≫ chartIncl d r J I hJ hI
1334	      = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom) := by
1335	  rw [chartTransition, chartIncl,
1336	    show CommRingCat.ofHom (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom

1348	-- The patch computation traverses the `pullbackDiagonalMapIdIso` / `pullbackSpecIso` instance

1351	open TensorProduct CategoryTheory.Limits in
1352	/-- The structure morphism `Gr(d,r) → Spec ℤ` is **separated** (`lem:gr_separated`, morphism form).
1353	Following the Proj template (`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.isSeparated`): on each patch
1354	`U^I ×_ℤ U^J` of `Pullback.openCoverOfLeftRight` the restricted diagonal is, via
1355	`pullbackDiagonalMapIdIso`, the affine morphism `Spec.map δ_{I,J}`, a closed immersion because

2154	      (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).map

2197	/-- **Section-level value of the affine base-change map.** In the affine–affine model — base ring
2198	maps `ψ : R ⟶ R'` (the base change `g = Spec ψ`) and `φ : R ⟶ A` (the affine morphism
2199	`f = Spec φ`), with `F = M^~` for an `A`-module `M`, the canonical pullback square supplying
2200	`g' = pullback.fst`, `f' = pullback.snd` — the global-sections incarnation `Γ(α)` of the
2201	base-change map `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is an isomorphism of `R'`-modules.
2202	
2203	By the four affine dictionaries the domain `Γ(g^*(f_* M^~))` reads as `R' ⊗_R M` and the codomain
2204	`Γ(f'_*(g')^* M^~)` reads as `(R' ⊗_R A) ⊗_A M`, under which `Γ(α)` is the canonical cancellation
2205	isomorphism `TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange` (in the orientation
2206	`Γ(α) = cancelBaseChange⁻¹`, `r' ⊗ m ↦ (r' ⊗ 1) ⊗ m`); since `cancelBaseChange` carries no
2207	flatness hypothesis, `Γ(α)` is an isomorphism. This is the section-level content of the affine
2208	close: it is the per-affine-chart input to `base_change_map_affine_local` once the locality
2209	reduction has restricted an arbitrary square to this affine–affine model. See blueprint
2210	`lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange`.
2211	
2212	The proof is the 4-step generator trace of the blueprint: unwind the adjoint mate of the
2213	`((g')^*, (g')_*)`-unit on global sections through `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` and
2214	`pullback_spec_tilde_iso`, landing on `cancelBaseChange⁻¹`. This theorem's **body has no inline
2215	`sorry`** (it conjugates `base_change_mate_generator_trace` back through the domain/codomain reads),
2216	but it is **transitively `sorry`-backed** through `base_change_mate_gstar_transpose`. The genuine
2217	crux — the mate-unwinding coherence over the generic pullback square
2218	`pullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)` — is the outstanding obligation carried by
2219	`base_change_mate_gstar_transpose` (and, beneath it, `base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs`), NOT
2220	here. -/
2221	theorem pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange
2222	    {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}} (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
2223	    IsIso ((moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).map
2224	      (pushforwardBaseChangeMap (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)
2225	        (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
2226	        (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
2227	        (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)).w (tilde M))) := by
2228	  -- Assemble the chain (blueprint `lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange`): the domain
2229	  -- read `Θ_src` (`base_change_mate_domain_read`) and codomain read `Θ_tgt`
2230	  -- (`base_change_mate_codomain_read`, resting on the `pullbackSpecIso` leg identification
2231	  -- `pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor`) identify `Γ(α)`'s domain/codomain with `R' ⊗_R M` and

There are 3 additional sorries: L2122 (`base_change_mate_gstar_transpose`), L2303 (`affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`), L2325 (`flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso`). Both are honest incomplete proofs, but the presence of two independent sorry sites in the conjugate chain means the path to an axiom-clean `flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso` is blocked at two points, not one. - `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean:2046–2047` — The phrase "dead `sorry`" referring to `_legs_conj`'s sorry is informal and ambiguous (could mean "abandoned" rather than "active-but-incomplete"). Minor clarity issue; the surrounding context resolves the ambiguity.

## Excuse-comments (always called out separately)

None found. No declaration in the audited files carries a comment of the form "TODO replace with real def", "placeholder", "temporary", "wrong but works", or "will fix later" attached to a load-bearing definition.

The sorry-backed proof comments are proof-plan comments (documenting what mathematical steps remain), not excuse-comments. They name the obstruction, the intended route, and why the sorry is there — which is the correct practice for documented incomplete proofs.

- **must-fix-this-iter**: 0

**Overall verdict**: The three audited files are mathematically honest. The 4 sorries in FlatBaseChange.lean are genuine incomplete proofs with documented plans, no statements have been weakened to avoid real obstructions, and the directive's specific verification targets (`Hfr`, `isProper_of_valuativeExistence`, `_legs`/`_legs_conj` split, conj-1a/1b/2c faithfulness) all pass. 41	
42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`

53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
54

79	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
80	   - Read the whole file.
81	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
82	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
83	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
84	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
85	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.

131	
132	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.
133	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).
134	- **Parallel APIs of existing Mathlib**: a project file that copy-and-modifies a Mathlib definition (changing one type-class or argument) instead of instancing the existing Mathlib version. Bridge lemmas needed to consume it are the symptom.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
151	
152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
153	
154	These run automatically between phases:
155	
156	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
157	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
158	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
159

9	/-!
10	# Flat base change for the pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf (`i = 0`)
11	
12	This file establishes the `i = 0` (direct-image) case of flat base change: the
13	formation of the pushforward `f_* F` of a quasi-coherent sheaf commutes with flat
14	base change on the target.
15	
16	Throughout we work with a (commutative, eventually cartesian) square of schemes
17	```
18	  X' --g'--> X
19	  |f'        |f
20	  v          v
21	  S' --g---> S
22	```
23	recorded by morphisms `f : X ⟶ S`, `g : S' ⟶ S`, `f' : X' ⟶ S'`, `g' : X' ⟶ X`
24	with `g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`, and `F : X.Modules` a sheaf of modules on `X`.
25	
26	The three main declarations are:
27	
28	* `AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardBaseChangeMap` — the canonical base-change map
29	  `g^*(f_* F) ⟶ f'_*((g')^* F)`, built as the adjoint mate of the unit of the
30	  `((g')^*, (g')_*)`-adjunction.
31	* `AlgebraicGeometry.affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` — for `f` affine and the
32	  square cartesian, the base-change map is an isomorphism (affine case: tensor
33	  associativity).
34	* `AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso` — for `g` flat and `f`
35	  quasi-compact quasi-separated, the base-change map is an isomorphism.
36	
37	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex`.
38	
39	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §"Cohomology and base change, I",
40	Tag 02KH.
41	-/
42	
43	universe u
44	
45	open CategoryTheory Limits
46	
47	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
48	
49	open Scheme.Modules
50	
51	open scoped TensorProduct
52	
53	variable {S S' X X' : Scheme.{u}}
54	  (f : X ⟶ S) (g : S' ⟶ S) (f' : X' ⟶ S') (g' : X' ⟶ X)
55	
56	/-- The canonical base-change map for the pushforward of a sheaf of modules.
57	
58	Given a commutative square
59	```
60	  X' --g'--> X
61	  |f'        |f
62	  v          v
63	  S' --g---> S
64	```
65	(with `comm : g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`) and a sheaf of modules `F` on `X`, this is the
66	canonical morphism `g^*(f_* F) ⟶ f'_*((g')^* F)` of sheaves of modules on `S'`.
67	
68	It is the image, under the `(g^*, g_*)`-adjunction transpose, of the composite
69	```
70	  f_* F --f_*(unit)--> f_* (g')_* (g')^* F
71	        = (g' ≫ f)_* (g')^* F = (f' ≫ g)_* (g')^* F = g_* f'_* (g')^* F,
72	```
73	where `unit` is the unit of the `((g')^*, (g')_*)`-adjunction and the middle
74	equalities are the pseudofunctoriality of pushforward together with the
75	commutativity of the square.
76	
77	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §"Cohomology and base change, I",
78	base-change diagram. -/
79	noncomputable def pushforwardBaseChangeMap (comm : g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g) (F : X.Modules) :
80	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F) ⟶
81	      (pushforward f').obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F) :=
82	  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).homEquiv _ _).symm
83	    ((pushforward f).map ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g').unit.app F) ≫
84	      (pushforwardComp g' f).hom.app _ ≫
85	      (pushforwardCongr comm).hom.app _ ≫
86	      (pushforwardComp f' g).inv.app _)

8	/-!
9	# The Quot scheme: Quot-foundations layer
10	
11	This file packages the Quot-foundations layer of the
12	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman Quot-scheme construction. It introduces the
13	per-fiber Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf, the Quot functor
14	`Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of `T`-flat coherent quotients, and the Grassmannian
15	*scheme* (Mathlib at the pinned commit carries only a linear-algebra
16	Grassmannian) together with its representability.
17	
18	The 4 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
19	
20	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` (def) — the
21	   **Hilbert polynomial function** `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]` of a coherent
22	   sheaf `F` on `X` over a finite-type `π : X ⟶ S` with respect to a
23	   line bundle `L`. Encoded as a function `S → Polynomial ℚ`.
24	
25	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor` (def) — the **Quot
26	   functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` sending an
27	   `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the set of equivalence classes
28	   `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with `F` a `T`-flat coherent sheaf on
29	   `X_T`, `q : E_T ↠ F` a surjection, and `F|_{X_t}` having Hilbert
30	   polynomial `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
31	
32	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian` (def) — the
33	   **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
34	   rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V`.
35	
36	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable` (theorem)
37	   — the **representability of the Grassmannian** by a smooth projective
38	   `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with
39	   the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
40	
41	## Note on type expressivity
42	
43	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
44	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
45	
46	- `hilbertPolynomial` returns `Polynomial ℚ` keyed by `s : S`, not
47	  `Unit`; the Hilbert polynomial is a non-trivial invariant of the
48	  coherent sheaf at the fiber over `s`.
49	- `QuotFunctor` and `Grassmannian` return contravariant functors into
50	  `Type u` — substantive presheaves of sets, not constant functors.
51	- `Grassmannian.representable` packages the
52	  `Functor.RepresentableBy` Yoneda-bijection structure: existence of a
53	  scheme `Y` together with a `RepresentableBy Y` witness — substantive
54	  content (a representable functor is determined by its representing
55	  object up to canonical isomorphism, and the witness is the data of
56	  that isomorphism family).
57

59	
60	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides:
61	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules` (the category `X.Modules`),
62	- `Scheme.Modules.pullback`, `Scheme.Modules.pushforward` (the

92	/-! ## §1. Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf
93	
94	For a finite-type morphism `π : X ⟶ S` with `S` noetherian and a coherent
95	sheaf `F` on `X` whose schematic support is proper over `S` (here encoded
96	as plain `X.Modules` for the file-skeleton), the per-fiber Hilbert
97	polynomial is the function
98	
99	`s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ],   Φ_{F,s}(m) = χ(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`.
100	
101	Snapper's Lemma ensures this is a polynomial in `m`; the proof requires
102	graded-Euler-characteristic infrastructure and is not stated here.
103	
104	Blueprint reference: `def:hilbert_polynomial` (Nitsure §1; cf. Hartshorne
105	III.5.2). -/
106	
107	/-- The **Hilbert polynomial** of a coherent sheaf `F` on `X` over `S` at
108	the fiber over `s ∈ S` with respect to a line bundle `L` on `X`.
109	
110	Encoded as a function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]`. The defining formula
111	
112	`Φ_{F,s}(m) = χ(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗ m})
113	            = Σ_i (-1)^i dim_{κ(s)} H^i(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`
114	
115	is a polynomial in `m` by Snapper's Lemma; the polynomial coefficients
116	depend on `s` through the fiber `F|_{X_s}`. When `F` is `S`-flat the
117	function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s}` is locally constant on `S`.

128	/-! ## §2. The Quot functor
129	
130	The Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` sends an `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the
131	set of equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` where
132	- `F` is a coherent sheaf on `X_T = X ×_S T` whose schematic support is
133	  proper over `T` and which is `T`-flat,
134	- `q : E_T ↠ F` is a surjective `O_{X_T}`-linear homomorphism,
135	- the fiberwise Hilbert polynomial of `F|_{X_t}` with respect to `L|_{X_t}`
136	  equals `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
137	
138	Two pairs `(F, q)` and `(F', q')` are equivalent iff `ker(q) = ker(q')`.
139	
140	The Hilbert scheme is the special case `E = O_X`:
141	`Hilb^{Φ,L}_{X/S} = Quot^{Φ,L}_{O_X/X/S}`.
142	
143	Blueprint reference: `def:quot_functor` (Nitsure §1; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
144	
145	/-- The **Quot functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of coherent quotients of `E`
146	on `X ×_S -` with Hilbert polynomial `Φ`.
147	
148	Encoded as a contravariant functor `(Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u`, sending an
149	`S`-scheme `T → S` (i.e. an object of `Over S`) to the set of
150	equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` of a `T`-flat coherent
151	sheaf `F` on `X ×_S T` with proper support and a surjection
152	`q : E_T ↠ F` whose fiberwise Hilbert polynomial is `Φ`, modulo
153	`ker(q) = ker(q')`. Functoriality is pullback of the quotient along
154	`X ×_S T' ⟶ X ×_S T`.
155	
156	iter-177+: the body packages the on-objects / on-morphisms data using the
157	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` bifunctor on the relative product
158	`X ×_S T`, with the equivalence relation `ker(q) = ker(q')` quotiented
159	out via `Setoid` / `Quotient`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body
160	is a typed `sorry`. -/
161	noncomputable def QuotFunctor {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
162	    (_π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType _π] (_L _E : X.Modules)
163	    (_Φ : Polynomial ℚ) :
164	    (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u :=
165	  sorry
166	
167	end Scheme

169	/-! ## §3. The Grassmannian scheme
170	
171	Since Mathlib carries no Grassmannian *scheme*, we encode it here as a
172	contravariant functor on `Over S` together with a representability
173	statement. The construction proceeds by gluing `binom(r, d)` affine
174	charts `U^I ≅ A^{d(r-d)}_S` along the Plücker cocycle, yielding a smooth
175	projective `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`,
176	equipped with a tautological rank-`d` quotient
177	`π* V ↠ U` and the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
178	
179	Blueprint references: `def:grassmannian_scheme`,
180	`thm:grassmannian_representable` (Nitsure §1 Exercise (2),
181	"Construction of Grassmannian"; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
182

185	/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
186	rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r ≥ d`.
187	
188	Encoded as the functor sending an `S`-scheme `T → S` to the set of
189	equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with
190	`q : V_T ↠ F` a surjection of `O_T`-modules and `F` locally free of
191	rank `d`, modulo `ker(q) = ker(q')`. Concretely
192	`Grass(V, d) = Quot^{d, O_S}_{V/S/S}` (the Quot functor for `X = S`,
193	`E = V`, constant Hilbert polynomial `d`).
194	
195	iter-177+: the body re-exports `QuotFunctor (𝟙 S) (?) V Φ_d`, where
196	`Φ_d : Polynomial ℚ` is the constant polynomial `d`. For the iter-176
197	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
198	noncomputable def Grassmannian {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
199	    (_V : S.Modules) (_d : ℕ) :
200	    (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u :=
201	  sorry
202	
203	/-- **Representability of the Grassmannian.**
204	
205	For a noetherian scheme `S`, a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r`,
206	and `1 ≤ d ≤ r`, the Grassmannian functor `Grass(V, d)` of
207	`Grassmannian` is representable by a smooth projective `S`-scheme
208	`Gr_S(V, d) ⟶ S` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with a
209	tautological rank-`d` quotient `π* V ↠ U`. The determinant line bundle
210	`det(U)` is relatively very ample, giving a Plücker closed embedding
211	`Gr_S(V, d) ↪ ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
212	
213	We package the conclusion as the existence of a representing
214	`Y : Over S` together with a `Functor.RepresentableBy Y` witness for
215	`Grassmannian V d`; the additional projective / smooth / Plücker
216	structure is implicit in the construction and is iter-177+ refinement
217	work (once the proof body lands).
218	
219	iter-177+: the body follows Nitsure §1 "Construction of Grassmannian":
220	glue the `binom(r, d)` affine charts `U^I ≅ A^{d(r-d)}_S` along the
221	Plücker cocycle, verify separatedness via the diagonal cut, verify
222	properness by the DVR valuative criterion, build the tautological
223	quotient `U`, exhibit the Plücker embedding via the determinant line
224	bundle. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
225	theorem Grassmannian.representable {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
226	    (V : S.Modules) (d : ℕ) :
227	    ∃ (Y : Over S), Nonempty ((Grassmannian V d).RepresentableBy Y) := by
228	  sorry
229	
230	end Scheme

241	/-- **Locally free of rank `d`** for a sheaf of modules on a scheme.
242	
243	A sheaf of modules `M` on a scheme `X` is *locally free of rank `d`* when `X`
244	admits an open cover `{U i}` on each member of which the restriction
245	`M|_{U i}` (the pullback of `M` along the open immersion `(U i).ι`) is
246	isomorphic to the free module of rank `d`, `O_{U i}^{⊕ d}` (encoded as
247	`SheafOfModules.free (ULift (Fin d))` over the structure-ring sheaf of the
248	open subscheme `(U i).toScheme`).
249

8	/-!
9	# Grassmannian affine charts
10	
11	This file contains the single blueprint-pinned declaration for the affine
12	charts of the Grassmannian scheme: `AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.affineChart`.
13	
14	For `I ⊆ Fin r` with `#I = d`, the affine chart `U^I` of the Grassmannian
15	`Gr(d, r)` is the spectrum of the polynomial ring in the `d(r-d)` free matrix
16	entries — `Spec ℤ[x^I_{p,q}]_{q ∉ I}` — which is non-canonically isomorphic
17	to `𝔸^{d(r-d)}_ℤ`.
18	
19	Blueprint reference: `def:gr_affine_chart`
20	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`).
21	-/
22

25	universe u

29	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
